outcome_text: The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable. Limitations of the generalizability beyond the conditions under which the technology was developed are documented.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system validity and reliability demonstration, including validation methodology, reliability testing, generalizability limitation identification, and documenting the boundaries of system applicability beyond development conditions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system validity and reliability demonstration, including validation methodology, reliability testing, generalizability limitation identification, and documenting the boundaries of system applicability beyond development conditions.
reference_document_element_description: Evaluate AI application performance against defined quality, safety, and reliability criteria, documenting test results and identified failure modes.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.5 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.735, cosine: 0.746). Secondary: RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.674), RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.645).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.3/10 (raw 0.735). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documenting, reliability]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [applicability, beyond, boundaries, conditions, demonstration, development] vs WIDAI terms [against, application, criteria, defined, evaluate, failure] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.875, R=0.884). Balanced P/R — the 26-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: notable entailment (99.1%). "Knowledge of AI system validity and reliability demonstration, including validat" shows logical implication toward "Evaluate AI application performance against defined quality, safety, and reliabi" — stronger than typical for cross-framework competency pairs, reinforcing the STS-based relationship.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.746 ≈ STS 0.735 (gap 0.011). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, documenting, reliability] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, documenting, reliability]. NIST AI RMF-only: [applicability, beyond, boundaries, conditions, demonstration, development]. WIDAI-only: [against, application, criteria, defined, evaluate, failure].
